CheckTitle: AI Agent Security Events Must Trigger Alerts Within 5 Minutes
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Observability**. Verifies that AI agent security events trigger automated alerts within 5 minutes. Without timely alerting, security incidents involving AI agents may go unnoticed for extended periods, dramatically increasing the blast radius and recovery cost of a breach.
Risk: Delayed detection of security events allows attackers extended dwell time to exfiltrate data, escalate privileges, or cause further harm. AI agents with access to sensitive systems and data require near-real-time alerting to limit the impact of compromises, prompt injection attacks, or credential abuse.

Code.Other:
1. Define security events to alert on: authentication failures, permission denials, anomalous API call rates, detected injection attempts, and policy violations.
2. Configure alerting pipelines with a maximum 5-minute detection-to-alert latency.
3. Route alerts to the security operations centre (SOC) or on-call team via PagerDuty, OpsGenie, or equivalent.
4. Test alert pipelines monthly with simulated security events.

Recommendation.Text: Implement automated security event alerting for all AI agent security-relevant events with a maximum 5-minute latency. Route alerts to an on-call security operations team and test alert pipelines regularly.

Notes: ASPM-082 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
